stimulus: In 1947, Japanese-born Washington resident Hiro Yamasaki became a leader in the Asian American civil rights movement. In March of that year, he won a landmark fair-housing case in the Washington Supreme Court. ______blank in October, he delivered a public address criticizing his local university for excluding Japanese American applicants. Both actions are remembered today as historic stands against discrimination.
stem: Which choice completes the text with the most logical transition?
rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. "Later" logically signals that the public address discussed in this sentence occurred later in a chronological sequence of events than did the fair-housing case discussed in the previous sentence.

Choice A is incorrect because "for instance" illogically signals that the address is an example of the court case. Instead, the address is an event that occurred after the case. Choice B is incorrect because "in other words" illogically signals that the address is a paraphrase of the court case. Instead, it is a later event. Choice C is incorrect because "rather" illogically signals that the address is an alternative to the court case. Instead, it is a later event.
